s1_reasoning: Each inner iteration performs 2 FLOPs (mul+add) but streams multiple operands from memory; without cache blocking/packing, arithmetic intensity is low and performance is limited by memory bandwidth and cache misses (especially for the right-hand matrices B, D, F accessed across k). With proper blocking/BLAS, it can become more compute-bound.

hotspots.location: kernel_3mm: E := A*B triple loop (around lines 86-96)
hotspots.reason: Dense matrix multiplication O(ni*nj*nk) with poor cache reuse for B[k][j] (stride-1 in j but k changes in inner loop) and high memory traffic; dominates runtime for typical PolyBench sizes.

hotspots.location: kernel_3mm: F := C*D triple loop (around lines 98-108)
hotspots.reason: Dense matrix multiplication O(nj*nl*nm); similar access pattern issues (D[k][j] accessed with k inner, causing repeated streaming of D columns) and large working set.

hotspots.location: kernel_3mm: G := E*F triple loop (around lines 110-120)
hotspots.reason: Dense matrix multiplication O(ni*nl*nj); typically comparable to the other two GEMMs and suffers from limited cache reuse without blocking.

hotspots.location: print_array (around lines 52-70) when enabled by PolyBench dump/prevent_dce
hotspots.estimated_time_percentage: 0-60% (depends on build/run configuration and output target)
hotspots.reason: I/O can dominate wall time if dumping is enabled or output is not redirected; not part of the timed kernel but can dominate end-to-end runtime.

bottleneck_type.reasoning: Each inner iteration performs 2 FLOPs (mul+add) but streams multiple operands from memory; without cache blocking/packing, arithmetic intensity is low and performance is limited by memory bandwidth and cache misses (especially for the right-hand matrices B, D, F accessed across k). With proper blocking/BLAS, it can become more compute-bound.

gpu_suitability.reasoning: The kernel consists of three large dense matrix multiplications, which map extremely well to GPUs (high parallelism, regular memory access after tiling). Performance is typically dominated by GEMM throughput; intermediate matrices E and F can remain on device to avoid PCIe transfers.
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Use cuBLAS/rocBLAS GEMM for the three multiplications. Allocate A,B,C,D,E,F,G on device, copy inputs once, run: E=A*B, F=C*D, G=E*F, then copy back G. Use appropriate leading dimensions and transpose flags for row-major data (or store matrices in column-major). Consider using streams and keeping intermediates on GPU; fuse nothing unless memory is constrained.
